results.query: DOGE Department of Government Efficiency instability fallout Q1 2026
results.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) was established in January 2025 but ceased to exist in November 2025, with its tasks assumed by the Office of Personnel Management. It faced legal challenges, lawsuits over data access, and disputes about its costs and savings, but no evidence of predicted instability or fallout emerged in Q1 2026. DOGE's temporary organization was scheduled to end on July 4, 2026, and workforce reductions occurred earlier without reported broader instability.[1][2]

results.query: California TikTok investigation AG findings Q1 2026
results.description: No evidence of findings released from a California TikTok investigation in Q1 2026 was found. A two-and-a-half-year investigation by California AG Rob Bonta culminated in a lawsuit against TikTok announced in October 2024, accusing the company of exploiting young users based on internal documents showing knowledge of addictive features and harms like depression and suicidal ideation. The lawsuit remains ongoing with no reported resolution or new findings by March 10, 2026.

results.query: Ro Khanna TikTok ByteDance investigation findings March 2026
results.description: No evidence was found of any investigation led by Rep. Ro Khanna into TikTok or ByteDance, nor any findings released in March 2026. Recent reports discuss a TikTok deal forming a new U.S. entity with ByteDance owning 19.9%, which Rep. Khanna addressed regarding creator uncertainty, but the House Select Committee on the CCP, chaired by Rep. Moolenaar, plans hearings on the deal. Khanna has focused on repealing the ban, protecting creators, and free speech rather than launching an investigation.

QXO secured $3 billion in financing in January 2026 to fund its acquisition strategy and was in talks with seven targets. On February 11, 2026, QXO agreed to acquire Kodiak Building Partners for $2.25 billion, expected to close in Q2 2026. Brad Jacobs indicated an active acquisition pipeline remains post-Kodiak.

results.query: Executive Order 14375 legal challenge International Organizations Immunities Act 2026
results.description: Executive Order 14375, issued on January 22, 2026, designated the Board of Peace as a public international organization entitled to privileges, exemptions, and immunities under the International Organizations Immunities Act (IOIA). No evidence of a specific legal challenge to EO 14375's IOIA authorization was found in search results. Related executive orders faced Supreme Court challenges on other grounds, such as IEEPA authority, but EO 14375 itself shows no reported litigation as of March 10, 2026.

Related activity on February 27, 2026, involved an NTEU letter contesting IRS termination of a collective bargaining agreement.

results.query: Schedule Policy Career federal employee reclassification implementation March 2026
results.description: The U.S. Office of Personnel Management (OPM) finalized the Schedule Policy/Career rule on February 6, 2026, which took effect on March 9, 2026. This rule established a new excepted service category for approximately 50,000 policy-influencing career federal positions, allowing at-will employment while preserving merit-based hiring. As of March 10, 2026, the rule was effective, enabling agencies to begin reclassifying positions via presidential executive order.

QXO filed its 10-K annual report for the year ended December 31, 2025, on February 27, 2026, one day after the predicted timeframe. The SEC EDGAR database lists the filing date as February 27, 2026. Company investor relations and Stock Titan confirm the 10-K was filed on that date.

Dogecoin has experienced volatility in Q1 2026, with price fluctuations between approximately $0.090 and $0.152, including a recent 15.1% surge. Technical analysis shows the asset stabilizing around support levels rather than sustained instability, with whale accumulation and reduced selling pressure indicating underlying support despite short-term price pressure.

Executive Order 14375, issued on January 22, 2026, designates the Board of Peace as a public international organization entitled to privileges and immunities under U.S. law. A legal challenge to this order has been raised, as documented in a Just Security article questioning the executive order's authority. No resolution or court ruling on this challenge has been identified in available sources as of March 10, 2026.

Israel issued a 60-day ultimatum for Hamas to disarm completely on February 16, 2026, with the timeline possibly starting around a February 19 meeting, threatening to resume military operations if unmet. Hamas rejected the ultimatum, stating it had not been notified and calling it baseless. As of March 10, 2026, approximately three weeks into the potential 60-day period, no resolution or compliance was reported.

A US-Israeli coalition launched extensive airstrikes on Iranian military targets, including missile production sites, IRGC headquarters, air defenses, and oil refineries, starting February 28, 2026, under Operation Epic Fury. Strikes continued through March 7, with over 3,000 targets hit by US CENTCOM and 300 by IDF in recent days, significantly degrading Iran's missile and drone capabilities. Iran retaliated with missile and drone attacks on Israel, US bases, and Gulf states like Saudi Arabia.
